item.title: Bulldogs star cleared of serious neck injury after frightening fall
item.description: The Western Bulldogs receive positive news from the scans on Aaron Naughton's neck after a frightening fall in Thursday night's loss to Sydney.

item.title: Danish two-train collision injures at least 18
item.description: The incident happened north of Copenhagen, with images showing two yellow and grey trains, both with visible damage.

item.title: Grapevine berry inner necrosis virus officially detected for first time in multiple Australian states
item.description: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

item.title: Marshall fumes at NRL refs as Koroisau faces hip-drop tackle verdict
item.description: Wests Tigers coach Benji Marshall has launched a passionate criticism of NRL officiating, following his side's 33-14 win over the Canberra Raiders.

item.title: Lance Collard's 'crippling' ban reduced to four weeks with two suspended after St Kilda appeal
item.description: St Kilda successfully appeals the AFL's nine-week ban handed to forward Lance Collard over a homophobic slur, reducing it to four weeks with two suspended.

item.title: Four 'finfluencers' suspected of giving unlawful advice hit with warning notices amid regulator crackdown
item.description: The warning notice to the so-called "finfluencers" relates to suspected provision of unlicensed financial advice, including promoting claims of guaranteed returns.

item.title: Matildas goalkeeper Chloe Lincoln on finding her voice and commanding her idols
item.description: The A-League Women finals kick off this weekend, with Brisbane to make its first appearance in five years.

item.title: Appeal board says homophobic and racist language commonplace in AFL due to 'highly competitive' nature of the game
item.description: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

item.title: Is it ever OK to use someone else's toothbrush?
item.description: About half of us have shared a toothbrush — usually between spouses, or parents and children. We asked the experts if it's a good idea, even in an emergency.

item.title: Italy dismisses Trump official's 'shameful' idea to replace Iran at football World Cup
item.description: Italian officials say they are not interested in replacing Iran at the FIFA World Cup, after the idea was raised by a Trump ally.

item.title: Resilient Queensland bush community keeps Eidsvold Cattle Drive alive
item.description: A dedicated band of volunteers is keeping a cattle drive tradition alive to raise money for vital health services.

item.title: US soldier charged with making $560,000 on Nicolás Maduro removal with Polymarket bets
item.description: In the weeks leading up to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro's January 3 capture, Gannon Ken Van Dyke used sensitive classified information to make wagers on prediction market Polymarket.

item.title: Dozen more Aboriginal burial sites detected amid construction on Rottnest Island
item.description: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

item.title: Benjamin Luke Johnston revealed as sniper who killed former bikie boss Nick Martin at Perth Motorplex
item.description: The former soldier who publicly assassinated ex-Rebels bikie boss Nick Martin at Perth Motorplex in 2020 can be publicly identified for the first time, after he successfully overturned a court order suppressing his identity.

item.title: Horse trainer wants Australia to remember light horsemen, their 'amazing' companions this Anzac Day
item.description: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

item.title: Fuel costs hit WA community sport as players skip games, clubs seek relief
item.description: Sports clubs and competitions are reporting lower participation, as players withdraw from training and competition due to high fuel and travel costs.

item.title: Defamation trial shown messages between Rebel Wilson and film producer
item.description: A Sydney court is shown a text message exchange between Hollywood star Rebel Wilson and a movie producer over a bathroom incident involving a young actor.

item.title: AFL 'specifically rejects' appeal board chair's comments regarding homophobic slurs after Lance Collard verdict
item.description: Both the AFL and the AFL Players' Association hit back at suggestions from the appeals board that slurs are commonplace among players in the sport.

item.title: Gas giant Woodside says additional tax on gas exports would kill projects at Senate inquiry
item.description: Oil and gas giant Woodside denies its business model is "weak" while arguing that paying a proposed flat tax on gas exports would kill the company's projects.

item.title: Berri Barmera councillor convicted of drink driving able to keep licence until after Anzac Day
item.description: Berri Barmera Councillor Ian Schlein will hand in his licence several days after a regional court convicted him of drink driving, to allow him to attend an Anzac Day parade.

item.title: Pickett takes mark of the year contender in Demon's stunning outing
item.description: Melbourne eases to a 54-point over Richmond at the MCG, as the hapless Tigers remain the competition's only side without a win in 2026. Follow live.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of The Shawshank Redemption, hair metal and Bugs Bunny
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Why Anzac Day Test between Australia and New Zealand means so much to Wallaroos star Piper Duck
item.description: Any time Piper Duck pulls on the Australian jersey is a special moment, but playing in the historic Anzac Day Test holds even greater significance for the Wallaroos back-rower.

item.title: Carlos Alcaraz out of French Open with wrist injury, Jannik Sinner urges him not to rush back
item.description: Two-time defending champion Carlos Alcaraz will not be able to defend his titles at Roland Garros due to a wrist injury that has friend and rival Jannik Sinner concerned.

item.title: Young Indigenous artist uses work to explore personal stories, experiences in care system
item.description: An Indigenous teenager is already making a name for herself as an artist, using her work to explore her identity and experiences in the care system.

item.title: Alex de Minaur beaten by Rafael Jodar in second round of Madrid Masters
item.description: Alex de Minaur loses another early-round match at the Madrid Masters, going down to local teen Rafael Jodar in straight sets as his clay court season struggles.

item.title: Bushfire survivors build fire-resilient house into Buchan hillside
item.description: During the Black Summer bushfires, the Grahams watched their house burn from a peephole in a fire bunker. When they decided to rebuild, they created the most fire-resilient home possible. Here's how they did it.

item.title: Aboriginal rock art discovered in remote Cape York during burn planning
item.description: Rangers have discovered an undocumented rock art site while conducting planned burns around existing sites.

item.title: Indigenous solar projects in Borroloola and Santa Teresa to tackle diesel shortage concerns and halve power bills
item.description: Two remote Indigenous communities have attracted a combined $11 million in funding for solutions to tackle their fuel security and cost-of-living crises.

item.title: Australia's weather forecasts are suddenly identical each day
item.description: Darwin to Melbourne, and everywhere in between, the seven-day forecasts reveal a monotonous similarity.

item.title: Blocking high to control eastern Australia's weather next week
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Farmers in WA food bowl region take $25 million cyclone hit
item.description: Growers across Western Australia's Gascoyne take a huge financial hit after four natural disasters in the first four months of 2026.

item.title: US Justice Department drops criminal investigation of Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell
item.description: The move could lead to a swift confirmation vote by the Senate for Kevin Warsh, a former top Federal Reserve official whom President Donald Trump nominated in January to replace Mr Powell.

item.title: This easy 'hug in a bowl' chicken stew is perfect for midweek dinners - ABC News
item.description: "It's a shortcut to that feeling that you get when you've slow cooked something all Sunday, but without that time or labour."

item.title: Cox's goal celebration sparks scuffle as Freo holds off Carlton comeback
item.description: Mason Cox gets in the face of Ben Ainsworth after kicking a fourth-quarter goal, resulting in some fireworks as Fremantle wins its sixth consecutive match.

item.title: Black Ferns outclass Wallaroos in Anzac Day Test
item.description: The Black Ferns produce a dominant performance to defeat the Wallaroos 40-5 in the first Anzac Day Test between the trans-Tasman rivals.

item.title: Ringo Starr brought country music to The Beatles — now he's getting back to it
item.description: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

item.title: Booing at Anzac Day services shows lack of public awareness of racism in Australia
item.description: Jeers could be heard for more than a minute as Uncle Ray Minniecon spoke at yesterday's Anzac service in Sydney with similar scenes playing out in Melbourne and Perth.

item.title: Dragons halfback Kade Reed to keep starting role, says interim coach Dean Young
item.description: Dean Young backs Kade Reed to remain St George Illawarra's halfback even after the Dragons slumped to a 62-16 thrashing in the 20-year-old's highly anticipated NRL debut.

item.title: Ollie Hollands a late out for Carlton after Elijah Hollands's struggles, but coach Michael Voss says he is 'in a good space'
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss says Elijah Hollands's younger brother Ollie is doing OK despite being a late out for the club's Anzac Day clash with Fremantle.

item.title: Thirty years after losing his family in the Port Arthur massacre, Walter Mikac says gun control is still vital
item.description: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

item.title: Why Shaun Micallef wrote 'gothic romance novel' De'Ath Takes a Holiday
item.description: After Shaun Micallef finished up 11 years hosting comedy news show Mad as Hell in 2022, the comedian did something unexpected: he wrote a 'gothic romance novel'.

item.title: Melbourne on track to record its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade
item.description: A blocking high has put Melbourne on track for its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade.

item.title: Mother of woman who died at Bandyup Women's Prison questions if daughter had adequate care
item.description: Deaths in custody are on the rise and family members and advocates for a woman who died in a Perth prison say more needs to be done to keep inmates safe.

item.title: Dawson fights back tears after return as GWS edge Roos amid ARC drama
item.description: Adelaide skipper Jordan Dawson breaks down in tears after playing his first match since his brother's tragic death, as GWS prevails against North with the aid of a major ARC blunder.

item.title: Manly's revival continues as Foran sets record, Panthers down Knights
item.description: Manly's revival under interim coach Kieran Foran has continued with a fourth straight win, as the Sea Eagles scored five tries in the second half to sink the Parramatta Eels.

item.title: Qld Premier David Crisafulli attends Anzac Day event with no Acknowledgement of Country
item.description: The event in Townsville, which has a proud military history and is Australia's largest garrison city, also did not include a Welcome to Country.

item.title: Veteran's advice after Anzac Day booing: Find your 'warrior spirit'
item.description: While condemnation has been swift after Indigenous elders were booed on Anzac Day, solutions to this type of racism seem harder to pin down. Elders have advice to share.

item.title: Why women are getting less sleep than men — and what we can do about it
item.description: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 27: Have you got the memory to beat our quiz?
item.description: Which Australian city played host to Expo 88? And what age did the world's oldest spider reach?

item.title: Why street artist Peter Drew decided to bring back his 'Aussie' poster series
item.description: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

item.title: Wet week ahead for south-east Qld due to unusually 'persistent' weather system
item.description: The "very slow-moving" high-pressure system causing the wet weather should "finally get a move on" early next week, the BOM says.

item.title: Cameron Green hits form at right time as Kolkata Knight Riders beat Lucknow Super Giants in extraordinary IPL clash
item.description: It's taken a while but Cameron Green is showing why he's worth the big pay packet as he helped Kolkata win an astonishing IPL Super Over affair against Lucknow.

item.title: Bad Company: Art and big business collide in new Anne Edmonds, Kitty Flanagan comedy
item.description: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

item.title: Sheep farmers swear by Lifetime Ewe course to steer them through drought and diesel price spikes
item.description: With a quick feel of a sheep's back, farmers score their flock, finding the magic numbers to help them navigate record-breaking drought, and now diesel price spikes.

item.title: Who is Cole Tomas Allen, Donald Trump's would-be assassin?
item.description: Details from the life of US President Donald Trump's would-be assassin paint a picture of a high achiever who took an abrupt turn towards political violence.

item.title: Kerry Kennedy thought of father's assassination when shooter rushed security at gala dinner
item.description: Human rights activist Kerry Kennedy praises a "superhero" member of Congress for shielding her when an active shooter attempted to storm the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

item.title: Indigenous veteran Di Ryder slams 'distracting' booing at Anzac Day Welcome to Country
item.description: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

item.title: Four easy egg recipes for any occasion
item.description: If you're looking to add the humble egg into your weekly meals, we have you covered.

item.title: RSL Australia to review Welcome to Country policies after elders booed on Anzac Day
item.description: RSL Australia will review its Welcome to Country guidelines after Indigenous elders were booed during Anzac Day services across the country.

item.title: AFL concedes error on controversial ARC call as league boss says umpiring 'as good as it's been for a long time'
item.description: AFL football boss Greg Swann says most of the feedback he receives from the clubs surrounding the officiating in the league is positive despite a pair of controversial review calls in Round 7.

item.title: The moment Andy Lee feared exploits with Hamish Blake had gone too far
item.description: Andy Lee opens up on his 23-year working relationship with his "fast and loose" comedy partner Hamish Blake, and the stunt that left him fearing for his mate's life.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Learn why tomorrow's inflation data could be important for you in just three minutes
item.description: There are some key moments coming up for the Australian economy and they could make a big difference to your bank account. Emily Stewart — aka Sensible Emily — is here to help you understand what to look out for.

item.title: Granddaughter of Port Arthur massacre's first victims speaks on 30th anniversary
item.description: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood delivers IPL masterclass as Royal Challengers Bengaluru smash Delhi Capitals
item.description: Australian pace maestro Josh Hazlewood delivers his best performance of the IPL season, taking four wickets to spearhead another RCB win.

item.title: Farmer rescues kangaroo from mud in dry dam as drought conditions hit western NSW
item.description: Photos show a kangaroo moments before being rescued from a dam in Central West NSW, after it became bogged in mud while searching for water in an increasingly barren landscape.

item.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
item.description: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

item.title: Indigenous players defend St Kilda coach Ross Lyon after report reveals comment during training session
item.description: Several indigenous players who have played under Saints coach Ross Lyon come to his defence after comments he made during a training session led to a meeting where Lyon offered to stand down.

item.title: Donald Trump calls for broadcaster to fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Broken PBS system behind failure to list Mounjaro for type 2 diabetics, according to Medicines Australia
item.description: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

item.title: Rebel Wilson describes herself as 'champion of women' as actor gives evidence in defamation trial
item.description: The Hollywood star is being sued for defamation in the Federal Court by Charlotte MacInnes, who plays the lead actor in Ms Wilson's debut directorial film The Deb.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Yarra River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Single-use plastic soy sauce fish are an eco-nightmare. A local designer has a solution
item.description: Shoyu-tai, the single-use plastic soy sauce fish that accompany takeaway sushi, are an eco-nightmare — but a local designer has a solution.

item.title: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop trio The Ronettes, dies aged 80
item.description: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.

item.title: Alex Johnston and Jarome Luai set to join PNG Chiefs in major get for NRL expansion team
item.description: Record-setting South Sydney try-scoring winger Alex Johnston looks set to become the first player to sign for the PNG Chiefs ahead of their 2028 NRL entry.

item.title: Farmers and authorities on alert as Australian plague locust migrates to South Australia
item.description: Multiple locust swarms in South Australia's Riverland region have put farmers and authorities on alert, prompting the South Australian Plague Locust Commissioner to send surveying teams to the region.

item.title: Locust swarms in the Murtho area
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Memorial service held to remember and reflect on Port Arthur massacre's enduring legacy, 30 years on
item.description: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

item.title: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa spared jail over child abuse material
item.description: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa is spared jail after a court found her novel fetishised young children.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball's powerful all-female statement on Anzac Day
item.description: They both struggle for recognition in male-dominated fields, so Super Netball chose to host an all-female Anzac Day ceremony to commemorate and give visibility to female veterans.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 29: Grab your ticket and a map, we're off on a quiz adventure
item.description: The Galápagos Islands are part of which South American country? The Elgin Marbles were taken from which ancient site?

item.title: First Nations film Wolfram writes hope into heavy history
item.description: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

item.title: Plastic packaging price pain could push up costs for shoppers on everything from milk to make-up
item.description: Companies that use plastic packaging are facing price pressures and supply disruptions, which they warn will likely mean higher costs for consumers. The cost of food-grade resin out of Singapore, which is used in milk bottles, has increased 110 per cent since the start of the Middle East war.

item.title: Amount of antibiotic florfenicol used at Tasmanian salmon farm lease concerning, say Greens, expert
item.description: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

item.title: Queenslanders urged to get tested for viral hepatitis after liver cancer deaths triple since 1980s
item.description: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

item.title: Former ASIO chief questioned scrutiny of Bondi gunmen before quitting royal commission
item.description: While advising the royal commission examining the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, the former head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency questioned whether early assessments of gunman Naveed Akram were sufficiently rigorous.

item.title: Western NSW dam limit increase plan dumped over impact on protected wetlands
item.description: Water users in part of the Murray-Darling Basin are anxious that a proposal to protect their interests during droughts has been abandoned, as the dry conditions continue to worsen.

item.title: King Charles addresses joint sitting of US Congress
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
item.description: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

item.title: Takeaways from King Charles's speech to the US Congress
item.description: In a speech that was supposed to be apolitical, King Charles hits on some hot-button issues that are fuelling controversies around the Trump administration.

item.title: Lions star Oscar Allen sidelined for 12-14 weeks with foot injury
item.description: The Lions' win over the Crows on Sunday has come at a price, with one of their major off-season signings rule out for much of the season.

item.title: Family of Queensland grandmother who died after weight-loss surgery says her death was preventable
item.description: Joanne Downie underwent an elective gastric bypass surgery so she could live a healthier life for her grandchildren, but she died six days later in a regional Queensland hospital.

item.title: UK parliament votes against inquiry into Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer over Peter Mandelson appointment
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer criticised the vote, calling it a political stunt by the Conservative Party timed to sway voters ahead of local and regional elections on May 7.

item.title: Jarome Luai confirms Wests Tigers exit to join PNG Chiefs for 2028 NRL season
item.description: Jarome Luai will leave Wests Tigers at the end of the 2027 season, announcing he will join the PNG Chiefs for their entry to the NRL.

item.title: Australian actor Megan Wilding stars in Game. Set. Match, a play that feels like tennis
item.description: What starts as a rom-com set at the Australian Open turns into a searing indictment of race relations in Australia in the new show Game. Set. Match.

item.title: Former FBI director James Comey indicted in probe over seashells photo
item.description: Former FBI director James Comey has been charged over a social media photo of seashells that officials say constituted a threat against US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Mother who faked son's eye cancer diagnosis to raise money sentenced to over four years' jail
item.description: A mother who faked her six-year-old son's cancer diagnosis to raise thousands of dollars has been jailed for more than four years for the "cruel, calculated" and "manipulative" crime.

item.title: Rebel Wilson accused of dumping her phone to avoid handing over key texts as part of defamation trial
item.description: Ms Wilson's lawyers have previously told Federal Court defamation hearing that her phone was stolen and she was unable to produce all of her WhatsApp message history.

item.title: Health Minister says Canberra Hospital knife incident will be subject to a joint review
item.description: The ACT's chief police officer says the knife produced by a Canberra Hospital patient that led to an hours-long security incident had not been detected by police during a search of the man earlier in the day.

item.title: Twenty-five-million-year-old platypus fossils with teeth found in outback SA
item.description: Researchers have discovered ancient platypus fossils in outback South Australia, where they are believed to have lived with lungfish, flamingos and freshwater dolphins. They also had major a difference from the modern species: well-formed teeth.

item.title: Keith Lees faces court over alleged murder of Meaghan Louise Rose on Sunshine Coast in 1997
item.description: Police allege Keith Lees killed Meaghan Louise Rose, whose body was found at the base of cliffs on the Sunshine Coast in July 1997.

item.title: New South Wales still committed to saving Tomago Aluminium as negotiations stall
item.description: The NSW government remains committed to Tomago Aluminium but is still waiting to learn how much the bailout will cost.

item.title: Brodie Dewar, accused killer of Labor identity Tim Picton, pleads not guilty to manslaughter
item.description: The man accused of killing former Labor identity Tim Picton outside a Northbridge venue has pleaded not guilty to his manslaughter.

item.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain
item.description: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

item.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped New South Wales into State of Origin shape
item.description: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.

item.title: AFL confirms draft changes despite 'disingenuous' opposition from clubs
item.description: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

item.title: Illicit cigarettes, vapes, cash seized in raids on Canberra's black market tobacco trade
item.description: The ACT's chief police officer wants a sustained crackdown on the black market tobacco trade, as authorities swoop on six Canberra businesses.

item.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
item.description: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

item.title: Threat to homes eases as fire near Uralla in NSW downgraded
item.description: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

item.title: SA Legislative Council result delay creating 'confusion and uncertainty'
item.description: With less than a week until state parliament resumes, an incoming upper house member says she still cannot access the building or appoint staff because the election result is yet to be declared.

item.title: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll opens up about mental health struggles in emotional post
item.description: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll reveals his mental health struggles in an emotional social media post, amid a push from players for the AFL to implement a mental health round.

item.title: Police explore link between alleged kidnappings and Melbourne hospitality attacks
item.description: Police say the trio are linked to targeted attacks on promoters of a weekly event held in South Melbourne.

item.title: Donald Trump's criticism of Australia unfair and harmful, says US Democrat Joe Courtney
item.description: US congressman Joe Courtney says the US president's repeated criticism of Australia is "unfair" and causing harm to the US amongst its allies.

item.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
item.description: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

item.title: 'Free Palestine' party pitched by YouTuber Avi Yemini to funnel votes to conservatives
item.description: Victorian voters are being urged to look out for "misleading" party names, as right-wing activists seek to redirect progressive votes to conservative parties.

item.title: Flag quiz, part 1: Are you a vexillologist in the making?
item.description: Do you know the Union Jack from the Stars and Stripes? And which flags aren't rectangular?

item.title: Gambling among Australian construction workers well above national average, creating safety risks
item.description: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

item.title: Matthew Pavlich and Steven Baker reflect on the infamous 'Sirengate' match in Launceston 20 years on
item.description: Two decades ago, two teams met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

item.title: Technology should make life easier but it doesn't always for people with disability
item.description: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

item.title: Anastasia Potapova reaches Madrid Open semifinals to make WTA Tour history
item.description: Anastasia Potapova experiences an "explosion of emotions", as her remarkable run in Madrid continues at the prestigious tournament.

item.title: NT diphtheria outbreak worsens as cases of the potentially deadly disease more than double
item.description: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

item.title: Six WA Police officers sanctioned over treatment of Aboriginal man who died in Kalgoorlie prison
item.description: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed at news of his death.

item.title: Australians urged to stand up for Murray River as 'transformational moment' looms
item.description: Julie Jones has lived by the River Murray all her life and wants all South Australians to participate in the first comprehensive review of the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.

item.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
item.description: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

item.title: Wastewater data suggests meth use is at a record high. Here's why we don't need to panic
item.description: At first glance, these findings sound alarming, but before we draw big conclusions about worsening drug use, it's worth taking a closer look at what this data can and can't tell us.

item.title: Perth wakes shrouded in thick fog as Main Roads issue traffic warning
item.description: Perth has been blanketed with thick fog, prompting warnings for motorists to use caution in the conditions.

item.title: Two men denied bail after allegedly flying meth in drone in Wollongong
item.description: Police allege a drone was used to deliver meth in Wollongong before it crashed, leading to a wider investigation.

item.title: Keith Lees committed to stand trial over alleged 1997 murder of Meaghan Louise Rose
item.description: Mr Lees will face a Supreme Court trial for allegedly killing Meaghan Louise Rose, whose death was originally suspected to be a suicide.

item.title: Archibald Packing Room Prize 2026 won by Sean Layh for portrait of Jacob Collins
item.description: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

item.title: Miranda Priestly makes a splashy return in The Devil Wears Prada 2
item.description: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

item.title: Ross Lyon clarifies Saints resignation offer over comment to First Nations players
item.description: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon is proud of the way the club reacted after he offended a group of the club's First Nations players.

item.title: Crows' skipper Jordan Dawson ruled out for Showdown after attending brother's funeral
item.description: Adelaide Crow's skipper Jordan Dawson has been ruled out of Friday's Showdown following his brother's sudden death.

item.title: Western Queensland's Pilungah Reserve third to gain special status ending mineral prospecting permits
item.description: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

item.title: NT Police find body believed to be missing 5yo Alice Springs girl
item.description: Northern Territory Police have found a body believed to be that of a missing five-year-old Alice Springs girl, five days after she disappeared from a town camp.

item.title: Parents of two children killed in Bowen Mountain house fire thank first responders and community
item.description: A statement shared by the parents of two children killed in a Bowen Mountain house fire say their "family's world has been forever changed".

item.title: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee on trial over Newcastle cocaine haul
item.description: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee is on trial over a cocaine shipment into Newcastle.

item.title: Victorian parliamentary inquiry into summer fires hears emotional pleas from Harcourt residents
item.description: A parliamentary inquiry into the devastating bushfires across Victoria over the summer hears the emotional plea of those impacted.

item.title: Rebel Wilson says social media posts are true during defamation trial cross-examination
item.description: Rebel Wilson tells a court several social media posts targeting the lead actor on her film The Deb are all true, on her final day of cross-examination in a defamation trial.

item.title: Ex-Vandemonian Touring music promoter Brendan Self charged with cocaine, cannabis offences
item.description: The former managing director of Hobart events company Vandemonian Touring appears in court charged with trafficking cocaine and possessing cannabis.

item.title: NSW Police under scrutiny over resources allocated to targeted Bondi Beach Hanukkah event
item.description: NSW Police officers assigned to patrol the Hanukkah celebration targeted in the Bondi Beach terrorist attack were told they did not need to "stay the entire duration" of the event, despite members of the Jewish community fearing it was at risk.

item.title: Water and sewerage bill increases on the way for Tasmanians
item.description: Water bills for Tasmanian households and small businesses will rise by an average of 5.7 per cent each year for the next four years.

item.title: Savers Australia denies allegations of bed bugs at Brunswick thrift store
item.description: Thrift store chain Savers Australia has denied there is evidence of bed bugs at their popular Brunswick shop, as allegations of an outbreak continue to circulate online.

item.title: NSW strikes late to win Game I as Origin series heads to Queensland
item.description: NSW has come from behind to win the opening match of the Women's State of Origin series, and their coach is looking forward to trying to win the shield in Queensland.

item.title: Moore goals after siren as Hawks and Pies settle for thrilling draw
item.description: A goal after the siren from Dylan Moore allows Hawthorn to claim a draw against Collingwood at the MCG.

item.title: NRL coach Craig Bellamy diagnosed with form of neurodegenerative disorder
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy has been diagnosed with a form of neurodegenerative disorder, the club has said.

item.title: WA Police seize guns from home with Nazi flags prominently displayed
item.description: Police raid a home as part of an operation to reassess licensed gun holders in the wake of the Bondi Beach shooting.

item.title: Community in mourning after 5yo girl's body found
item.description: Tributes are flowing in Alice Springs for a five-year-old Aboriginal girl whose body was found earlier today, as the community mourns her death.

item.title: Guy Montgomery went from lazy school student to hosting a TV spelling bee across two countries
item.description: Guy Montgomery is back with the third season of his inimitable Spelling Bee, with brand-new sidekick Sam Campbell. We turn the tables on the Kiwi comic to ask him the tough questions.

item.title: Seasonal produce for all your autumn soups, roasts and salads
item.description: From carrots to cauliflower, here is some of the best seasonal produce to enjoy in your autumn soups, roasts and salads.

item.title: ACCC case against Woolworths winds up as spotlight remains on integrity of grocery pricing
item.description: As the Woolies federal court case wraps up and the Coles matter awaits judgment, here's what we've learned so far from the high-stakes cases.

item.title: Cole Tomas Allen to remain in jail after alleged attempt to storm correspondents' dinner
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, appears briefly in court, days after he allegedly attempted to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner armed with guns and knives.

item.title: The Bondi royal commission interim report is careful but ultimately damning
item.description: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

item.title: National average house prices rose slightly in April, the slowest pace of growth since January 2025
item.description: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

item.title: At a Melbourne hospital, nitrous oxide users are presenting with psychosis
item.description: Harms from nitrous oxide have been growing in Australia in recent years, with the substance linked to a growing number of accidental deaths, while a study from a Melbourne hospital shows abuse of the inhalant is associated with psychosis.

item.title: ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions led by miners, ANZ shares slump despite profit jump — as it happened
item.description: The ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions but ANZ's profit announcement drags down the banks.

item.title: Extreme heat the most deadly yet preventable natural disaster, NSW Net Zero Commission says
item.description: An agency set up by the NSW government just two years ago says extreme heat is the most deadly yet most preventable natural hazard facing Australians.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood smashed for 24 off one over as Shubman Gill fires in the IPL
item.description: Shubman Gill takes Australian quick Josh Hazlewood to task as Gujarat took down Bengaluru in the IPL.

item.title: Ashley Youth Detention Centre cameras offline during lockdown incident
item.description: Security cameras at Ashley Youth Detention Centre were offline when a detainee threatened a staff member with a knife during a cooking workshop, documents reveal.

item.title: Left in the red dirt: The forgotten trail of Australia's longest strike
item.description: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

item.title: Reza Adib, partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, charged with rape
item.description: Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, has been charged with three counts of rape relating to alleged offending against a woman in her 30s at Burleigh Heads on March 30.

item.title: Remote patient monitoring technology saving time, travel and money for regional mums during pregnancy
item.description: Remote health technology is saving regional patients time and money, despite many Victorian hospitals still running on paper records.

item.title: Britney Spears charged with driving under influence of alcohol and drugs
item.description: Britney Spears has been charged in California with driving under the influence of drugs and alcohol.

item.title: Construction ends on $915 million Parramatta Powerhouse museum set to open this year
item.description: Four years after work started, the highly anticipated Western Sydney museum is ready for the installation of five exhibitions, including a major show about space exploration.

item.title: Adelaide showed how good LIV Golf could be, but it was built on a flawed premise
item.description: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

item.title: Townsville man dies in fiery crash as road fatalities jump 24pc
item.description: Members of the public and emergency services assisted at the scene of a fatal crash in Townsville, one of several deadly crashes on Queensland roads over the past week.

item.title: From Foo Fighters to Jessie Ware and Nine Inch Noize — these are April's best albums
item.description: Come for Dave Grohl and co's latest. Stay for club-facing collabs, lush disco, homegrown releases and a pair of Québécois aliens making left-field instrumental jams.

item.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan
item.description: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

item.title: Mel C on her new album Sweat and the Spice Girls
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Poliovirus linked to overseas transmission detected in Perth wastewater
item.description: Poliovirus has been detected in a sample taken from a wastewater in Perth, the Health Department reveals.

item.title: AFL score reviews under scrutiny with some coaches questioning the point
item.description: Nobody wants to see their team robbed by a missed officiating decision. But as scrutiny over video assistant refereeing or umpiring continues, some are wondering whether it's all worth it.

item.title: Billions of NDIS funds lost to 'integrity leakage' last year, executive tells parliamentary hearing
item.description: About $3.7 billion of National Disability Insurance Scheme money was handed out inappropriately last financial year, the scheme's integrity chief has told a parliamentary hearing.

item.title: Wollongong tradesman jailed for robbing paedophile in Lightning Ridge heist
item.description: A Wollongong man drove to Lightning Ridge with his police officer friend to rob an elderly convicted paedophile of $100,000.

item.title: Family of allegedly murdered 5yo calls for calm as police reveal vigilante attack left suspect unconscious
item.description: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

item.title: Review finds fire ant eradication can succeed if flaws in 'mistrust and misinformation' are fixed
item.description: An independent review finds fire ant eradication is still achievable, but major issues, including poor communication, public mistrust and strategy confusion, must be fixed first.

item.title: Beaches closed near Wollongong as whale carcass removed at Bellambi
item.description: The sperm whale carcass was towed from Era Beach in the Royal National Park to Bellambi Boat Ramp in Wollongong this afternoon.

item.title: Former ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker talks to Stateline
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of an iconic album cover, a shredding Banksy and that creepy dancing baby gif
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Gas vs beer: How David Pocock's 57-second video caused the government a weeks-long tax headache
item.description: David Pocock was struggling to find cut-through on the issue of gas taxes, but then a question about the beer excise sparked a social media war of words.

item.title: What is a town camp? The site of Kumanjayi Little Baby's tragic alleged abduction
item.description: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

item.title: Albanese says US under Trump 'playing a different role' in world
item.description: Anthony Albanese says the United States is "playing a different role" in the world under President Donald Trump, forcing Australia and allies to adjust how they respond to unexpected conflicts.

item.title: Starc takes 3 wickets on IPL return to halt Delhi Capitals' losing run, Labuschagne leads Hyderabad to PSL final
item.description: Mitchell Starc picked up three wickets on his first bowl since January's BBL final, reviving Delhi Capitals, while Marnus Labuschagne led Hyderabad United to the PSL final.

item.title: SA Liberals poised to hold firm in upper house as Dennis Hood rejects presidency role
item.description: Labor's path to getting legislation through the upper house may get slightly more difficult after Liberal MLC Dennis Hood — who was strongly linked to the upper house presidency role — said he would remain on the floor of parliament.

item.title: Iranian and FIFA football officials to meet ahead of World Cup games in USA
item.description: Iranian soccer officials are set to meet with FIFA in Zurich about their FIFA World Cup games in the United States in June, with Iran's football boss saying there are "many issues to discuss".

item.title: Australian cows help rebuild Indonesian dairy herds after foot-and-mouth outbreak
item.description: An Australian dairy program in Indonesia is helping farmers in West Java boost production after a devastating foot-and-mouth disease outbreak.

item.title: Inquiry into Queensland CFMEU branch hears of union's impact on construction of Brisbane's Cross River Rail
item.description: Queensland's inquiry into the CFMEU has revealed Cross River Rail was plagued by years of interference. Here are the key takeaways from this week's hearings.

item.title: Oscar Piastri third for Miami Sprint Race and Lando Norris on pole
item.description: Lando Norris starts the Miami Grand Prix sprint race on pole position, with world championship leader Kimi Antonelli denying Oscar Piastri a spot on the front row of the grid.

item.title: Number of Tasmania Police officers off duty due to mental health doubles in five years
item.description: More than 100 Tasmanian police officers are currently off duty due to mental health compensation claims, a number that has doubled in five years.

item.title: Restoring wetland habitat on cattle grazing property in Mackay
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Removing the bund at Cape Palmerston National Park
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How salt water is restoring life to parts of the Queensland coast
item.description: More than half a century ago, lush wetlands along the Queensland coastline were cut off from the ocean as farmers built physical barriers to stop salt water flowing into their land. Now, conservation groups and traditional owners are restoring tidal flows and bringing life back to the land.

item.title: Meekatharra bike donation and workshop gets kids cycling to school
item.description: The Meekatharra community comes together to restore donated bikes, making a visible difference at the local school.

item.title: XPT retro revival, as iconic train returns to the tracks, after major refurbishment.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Carlton blows another half-time lead as Voss pins blame on defensive effort
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss has said his side's defence is not up to standard after the Blues suffered another second-half collapse on Saturday.

item.title: Student rocket team successfully launches Galah rocket 10,000 feet in NSW outback
item.description: Students with space industry aspirations successfully launch a rocket 10,000 feet in the air in regional Australia.

item.title: Man airlifted with life-threatening injuries after reports of caravan park brawl involving knives
item.description: A man has been airlifted to hospital with life-threatening injuries after being stabbed during a wild brawl at a caravan park in a small Victorian town.

item.title: WA's energy minister sits down for a Stateline interview to discuss the state's fuel supply.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Spirit of Tasmania V arrives in Geelong as preparation for Bass Strait voyages continues
item.description: Following a seven-week journey from Scotland, the Spirit of Tasmania V has arrived in Geelong. It will undergo gantry testing tomorrow before being temporarily relocated to Station Pier in Melbourne.

item.title: Counting underway in Nepean by-election, with Liberal Party looking to retain Victorian state seat
item.description: The Liberal Party seeks to retain the seat vacated by former Victorian deputy Liberal leader Sam Groth.

item.title: Cody Ramsey on the field for the Roosters
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.
